main_doability_needs: bed-level or seated options by default
oxygen/breathing, pain, vertigo, fatigue, mobility, and recovery time respected
ADHD/autism supports: transitions, low-stimulation starts, tiny visible steps
partial completion and stopping before a crash count as success
no shame, no body blame, no pressure to push through pain or dysphoria
ADHD transition support before task demands
caregiver burnout recovery treated as care, not avoidance
protected rest, boundaries, decompression, sensory needs, and people-pleasing checks
no assumption that Jasper has unlimited caregiver capacity

household_rules: no hour block may hold more than two tasks/tools
skills may be used as one-breath or one-field versions
survival days load critical care first and skip optional worksheets
rest, boundaries, decompression, and asking for help count as care
skills are for support, not grades

Overview: Reality Acceptance Skills These are skills for how to live a life that is not the life you want. Radical Acceptance
Radical Acceptance (When you cannot keep painful events and emotions from coming your way.)
Radical Acceptance: Factors That Interfere Radical Acceptance Is Not: Approval, compassion, love, passivity, or against change. Factors That Interfere with Acceptance
Practicing Radical Acceptance Step by Step   Observe that you are questioning or fighting reality (“It shouldn’t be this way”). 
Turning the Mind Turning the mind is like facing a fork in the road. You have to turn your mind toward the acceptance road, and away from the road of rejecting reality.
Willingness Willingness is readiness to enter and participate fully in life and living. Find a willing response to each situation. Willingness is doing just what is needed:
Half-Smiling and Willing Hands Accepting reality with your body. Half-Smiling 1st. Relax your face from the top of your head down to your chin and jaw.
Practicing Half-Smiling and Willing Hands
Mindfulness of Current Thoughts
Practicing Mindfulness of Thoughts
Overview:

Common Addictions In case you thought you had no addictions, here is a list. You are addicted when you are unable to stop a behavior pattern or use of substances, despite negative consequences and despite your best efforts to stop.
Dialectical Abstinence Synthesis = Dialectical Abstinence The goal is not to engage in addictive behavior again— in other words, to achieve complete abstinence.
Planning for Dialectical Abstinence Plan for Abstinence   1. Enjoy your success, but with a clear mind; plan for temptations to relapse.
Clear Mind Addict mind is: Impulsive One-minded
Behavior Patterns Characteristic of Addict Mind and of Clean Mind Addict Mind 
Community Reinforcement Community reinforcement means replacing addiction reinforcers with abstinence reinforcers. Reinforcing Abstinence Is Critical
Goals of Distress Tolerance Survive Crisis Situations Without Making Them Worse Accept Reality

Burning Bridges and Building New Ones Burning Bridges Accept at the most radical level that you are not going to engage in addictive behavior again, and then move actively to cut off all addictive behavior options.
Alternate Rebellion and Adaptive Denial Alternate Rebellion
Overview: Crisis Survival Skills These are skills for tolerating painful events, urges, and emotions when you cannot make things better right away.
You are in a crisis when the situation is:
STOP Skill Stop Do not just react. Stop! Freeze! Do not move a muscle! Your emotions may try to make you act
Pros and Cons

Ending Relationships A destructive relationship has the quality of destroying or completely spoiling either the quality of the relationship or aspects of yourself—such as your physical body and safety, your self-esteem or sense of integrity, your happiness or peace of mind, or your caring for the other person.

Overview: Walking the Middle Path Balancing Acceptance and Change Dialectics
Dialectics Dialectics reminds us that
  1. There is always more than one side to anything that exists. Look for both sides.   Ask Wise Mind: What am I missing? Where is the kernel of truth in the other side?
Examples of Opposite Sides That Can Both Be True   1. You can want to change and be doing the best you can, AND still need to do better, try harder, and be more motivated to change.
Important Opposites to Balance   1. Accepting reality AND working to change it. 
